name: Cofrentes Castle Loop from Requena
name_original: El Castillo de Cofrentes — Vuelta desde Requena
description: This difficult 47.9-mile (77.0 km) road cycling route from Cofrentes to Requena features 3445 feet (1050 metres) of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Requena traverse a diverse landscape in the Valencia region of Spain, characterized by expansive vineyards, natural parks, and river canyons. The area features quiet roads and varied terrain, including rolling hills and more challenging mountain ascents. Cyclists can explore the Utiel-Requena wine region, the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park, and the Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Park. Best road cycling routes around Requena The most popular road cycling route is Climb to El Negrete pass — Circular from Requena, a 34.4 miles (55.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Parque Natural Chera-Sot de Chera — Tour from Requena, a difficult 49.0 miles (78.9 km) path. This route leads through the natural park, offering scenic views and challenging climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Cofrentes Castle Loop from Requena, a 47.9 miles (77.0 km) trail leading through the region's varied landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Road cycling around Requena is defined by vineyards, natural parks, and river valleys, offering a network of routes for various ability levels. The routes in Requena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Requena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Requena?
A: Road cycling routes around Requena traverse a diverse landscape. You'll find expansive vineyards, natural parks like Hoces del Cabriel and Chera-Sot de Chera, and river canyons. The area features quiet roads with varied terrain, including rolling hills and more challenging mountain ascents, offering hundreds of kilometers of scenic routes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Requena?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Requena, catering to various skill levels. These include easy vineyard loops, moderate rides with significant elevation, and difficult routes through natural parks and mountainous areas.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Requena?
A: Yes, Requena offers several routes suitable for beginners. While many routes involve elevation, there are at least 4 easy routes available. These often include leisurely spins through the vineyards, providing a gentler introduction to the region's cycling. For example, the 'Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop' is an easy 19.6-mile route mentioned in the region research.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes in the Requena area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Requena offers numerous difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843703186/parque-natural-chera-sot-de-chera-tour-from-requena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural Chera-Sot de Chera — Tour from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, a 49-mile path with significant climbs. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33850815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mijares River – Sandwich with Red Wine loop from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 100 km and features substantial elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Requena?
A: While road cycling in Requena, you can encounter a variety of natural features and landmarks. The routes often pass through the Utiel-Requena wine region with its expansive vineyards. You might also see the dramatic canyons of the Cabriel and Júcar rivers, or explore areas within the Hoces del Cabriel and Chera-Sot de Chera Natural Parks. Specific highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ropé&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5359267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de la Garita&lt;/a&gt;, and viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Júcar River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites to visit along road cycling routes in Requena?
A: Yes, Requena is rich in history. Many routes pass near or through the historic town of Requena itself, where you can explore its medieval quarter, La Villa. Notable historical landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4795711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Requena Fortress&lt;/a&gt; and the ancient Cuevas de la Villa. You might also encounter other historical structures like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2751423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chirel Castle&lt;/a&gt; on some routes.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes starting from Requena?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Requena are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e858765492/climb-to-el-negrete-pass-circular-from-requena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to El Negrete pass — Circular from Requena&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.4-mile route. The region research also mentions the 'Cofrentes Castle Loop from Requena' as another circular option.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Requena?
A: The Valencia region, including Requena, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with milder temperatures ideal for longer rides. The autumn months are especially scenic as the vineyards change color. While winter conditions are generally mild, summer can be very hot, so early morning rides are recommended during that season.

Q: Are there opportunities for coffee stops or refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, the Utiel-Requena wine region is known for its hospitality. Many routes pass through charming villages and towns where you can find cafes, restaurants, and wineries for coffee stops, refreshments, or even wine tastings. The region research specifically mentions 'convenient coffee stops' as a highlight for cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Requena?
A: The road cycling experience in Requena is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, quiet roads, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels. The combination of vineyards, natural parks, and historical sites also contributes to a unique and enjoyable experience.

Q: Is parking available near the starting points of road cycling routes in Requena?
A: Requena, being a town well-suited for outdoor activities, generally offers parking options. Many routes start directly from Requena or nearby villages, where public parking is usually available. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking facilities.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Requena?
A: While many road cycling routes in Requena involve some elevation, there are easier options that can be suitable for families, particularly those with older children or more experienced young riders. The flatter sections through the vineyards, such as parts of the 'Requena and Utiel via Camino de la Lana Loop', offer a more relaxed experience. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.
tips: This stretch of road is a real blast. From Requena in the direction of Cofrentes, the route runs down a paved surface in excellent condition, allowing us to appreciate wonderful views until we reach Casas del Rio.

It is a castle of Islamic origin dating from the 11th century built on one of the chimneys of the Cerro Agrás volcano and on the banks of the Cabriel river. It can be visited daily by appointment at the tourist office.

faqs.question: How challenging is the El Castillo de Cofrentes — Return from Requena route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a difficult road cycling experience. It spans approximately 77 kilometers with a significant elevation gain of 1,050 meters, requiring a very good level of fitness. While mostly well-paved, be prepared for some potentially unpaved and difficult segments where you might need to dismount.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The most significant highlight is the Cofrentes Castle, an 11th-century Islamic fortress perched on a volcano, offering impressive panoramic views. You'll also experience a Brilliant descent to Casas del Rio, and enjoy diverse scenery including vineyards, natural parks, and traditional villages, especially along the Cabriel River.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this road cycling route is approximately 4 hours and 33 minutes. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, speed, and time spent enjoying the sights.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Valencia region generally offers pleasant cycling weather for much of the year. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Summers can be very hot, so early morning rides are recommended if cycling during July or August.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes area?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for road cycling on public roads and trails in the Valle de Ayora-Cofrentes area. However, if you plan to visit the Cofrentes Castle, it typically requires an appointment and may have an entrance fee.

faqs.question: Is the El Castillo de Cofrentes — Return from Requena route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for race bikes on roads, and due to its length, difficulty, and traffic considerations, it is generally not suitable for cycling with dogs. It's best to leave your canine companions at home for this particular adventure.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access public transport near the start of the route in Requena?
faqs.answer: Requena is well-connected by road, and parking can typically be found within the town. For public transport, Requena has a train station (Requena-Utiel) with connections to Valencia, and bus services that can bring you to the town center, which serves as a good starting point for the route.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and attractive scenery. The route passes through the Requena region, known for its vineyards, and the Cofrentes area, rich in natural and cultural heritage. You'll see agricultural landscapes, natural parks, traditional villages, and spectacular views of the Júcar and Cabriel rivers amidst mountains.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several official trails. Notable sections include parts of La Chirrichana (29% of the route), Las Aldeas de Basta y Alcance (13%), El Campichuelo (10%), and Los Cañones del Júcar (5%), among others. This means you'll be sharing segments with other designated paths.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or rest stops along the El Castillo de Cofrentes — Return from Requena route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through villages, specific cafes or dedicated cycling rest stops are not guaranteed at frequent intervals. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's length and difficulty. You may find small shops or bars in the towns you pass through, such as Cofrentes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring a well-maintained race bike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, plenty of water, energy gels or snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), a fully charged phone, and navigation tools. Sun protection is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Brilliant descent to Casas del Rio
